codeReview.summary: Drop-screen behavior is mostly implemented, but the multi-file drop path does not satisfy the feature's graceful-handling requirement for mixed file sets.

issues.description: Multiple-file handling always processes `files[0]` and provides no one-file-at-a-time feedback. If a user drops multiple files where the first is non-PDF and a later file is a valid PDF, the valid PDF is ignored and the flow fails VAL-DROP-006's requirement to accept the first valid PDF (or show an explicit one-file message). See `handleDrop` at lines 99-105.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The ui-worker procedure requires reading `.factory/library/architecture.md`, but this step was skipped while handoff skillFeedback still marked procedure as fully followed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill requirement: `.factory/skills/ui-worker/SKILL.md:20`. Transcript skeleton for session `6fb5f3b3-44a3-4901-9190-1ea41606fdd3` shows reads of AGENTS, services, and `_design/homepage.html`, but no read of `architecture.md` (only directory listing at line 82).

sharedStateObservations.observation: State is mutated directly in the component (`error.value`, `appState.value`) instead of going through `dispatch`, but this dispatch-only rule is documented in `state.ts` rather than AGENTS conventions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `src/components/DropScreen.tsx:54-55,102` directly set signals; `src/app/state.ts:102-105` says all state transitions go through `dispatch(event)`; AGENTS.md does not explicitly state this as a project convention.

summary: Review result: fail. The feature misses a required multi-file edge case in drop handling, and there are shared-state/process documentation gaps around skill procedure adherence and dispatch-only state updates.
